title: Fix Trailing Whitespaces in String Concatenation

statement.markdown:
Your task is to fix the provided code so that it correctly concatenates two strings while removing any leading or trailing whitespaces.
 
You should modify the code to handle cases with non-whitespace characters at the beginning or end of the strings.
 
Note: You can assume that the input strings only contain printable ASCII characters.
 
**Input Format**: Two-space-separated strings
 
**Output Format**: Concatenated string without leading or trailing whitespaces
statement.outputFormat: Concatenated string without leading or trailing whitespaces
statement.notes: Time and Space complexity should be O(n), where n is the total number of characters in both strings

examples.input:
Hello World   
   This is a test
examples.explanation: The code should remove the leading and trailing whitespaces from the input strings

hints.text: Use the strip() function to remove leading/trailing whitespaces

editorial.title: Editorial for Fix Trailing Whitespaces
editorial.intuition: The code should use a string method to remove leading/trailing whitespaces while concatenating two strings
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would involve manually iterating over the characters in both strings to remove leading/trailing whitespaces
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution involves using the strip() function provided by the language
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using the strip() function and concatenating the strings using the + operator
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: A detailed explanation of why the provided code is correct

starterCode.javascript: function concatenate(a, b) { return a + b; }

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function concatenate(a, b) { return a + b + " "; }

functionSignature.cpp: string concatenate(const string& a, const string& b);
functionSignature.python: def concatenate(self, a: str, b: str) -> str:

testCases.input:
Hello World   
   This is a test
